sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The M squares moved towards the blob. Each M square moved 1 cell closer to the blob center (27, 33). The M squares are now at:
- (35-36, 16-17) - moved from (36-37, 17-18)
- (38-39, 35-36) - moved from (39-40, 36-37)
- (54-55, 45-46) - moved from (55-56, 46-47)
- (55-56, 14-15) - moved from (56-57, 15-16)
The level is not completed. The blob is still 69 cells.
I need to keep moving the M squares towards the blob until they reach it. Let me click them again to move them further.
Actually, in the previous attempt, the M squares moved towards the blob but stopped before reaching it, and then exploded. But maybe I need to keep clicking them until they reach the blob.
Let me click all 4 M squares again to move them further towards the blob.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: M squares moved 1 cell closer to blob. Level not completed. Need to keep moving them towards the blob. Let me click them again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find M squares on the board (not in top panel)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
m_squares = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 5]

# Click all 4 M squares again
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': n['boundary'][0][0], 'col': n['boundary'][0][1]} for n in m_squares]
result = action(actions)

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
m2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'M']
p2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
blob2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b']
print(f"M={len(m2)}, p={len(p2)}")
if blob2:
    print(f"Blob: {blob2[0]['pixels']}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
for n in m2:
    if n['boundary'][0][0] > 5:
        print(f"  M: {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
